name: Kasha Stuffed Tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------------KASHA STUFFING-------------------------------
1/2 cup Scallions, thinly sliced
2 garlic cloves, minced
1 celery rib, chopped
1 cup Mushrooms, chopped
2 tablespoons Olive oil
1 tablespoon Egg replacer, powdered
1/4 cup Water
1 cup Uncooked kasha
2 cups Hot vegetable broth
1/4 cup Wheatgerm
1/4 cup Pine nuts, chopped
1/4 cup Italian parsley, chopped
1/2 teaspoon Thyme
STUFFING: In a large pot, sauté scallions, garlic, celery, and mushrooms in 4 tablespoons olive oil until softened, about 5 minutes.
In a medium-sized bowl, whisk the egg replacer and water. Stir in the kasha, mixing well. Add the kasha mixture to the mushroom mixture, stirring to separate the grains. Add the hot broth and tomato pulp. Simmer for 2 to 3 minutes.
Cover and simmer until the broth is absorbed, about 8 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in the wheatgerm, pine nuts, parsley, and thyme.
ASSEMBLY: Preheat oven to 350°F and oil a shallow baking dish.
Arrange the tomato shells in the dish and fill with stuffing. Bake for 20 minutes.
Place reserved tops on the tomatoes, brush with the remaining oil. Bake another 10 minutes and then serve immediately.
